assertionResults.reason: The headed hero uses a real vendored Radiant Fluid Amber shader asset from shipped code, and the visible moving background can be traced directly to that vendored asset path rather than to a placeholder or comment-only citation.

observations: Headed DOM inspection on the live page reported data-visual-state='enhanced', data-motion-layers='2', data-canvas-ready='true', shaderCanvas=true, and shaderOpacity='0.85'.
The hero screenshot shows the animated blue fluid field visibly moving behind the BETTER headline and CTA stack; it reads as a shipped background layer, not a static gradient placeholder.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output only showed the React DevTools development notice and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional network or API requests were captured during settled hero validation.

assertionResults.reason: The shipped ASCII layer is rendered by a real-time Hermes-derived canvas renderer with glyph palettes, screen-blended multi-grid composition, and feedback logic, while the legacy DOM <pre> grid remains hidden in the enhanced state.

observations: Live DOM inspection reported asciiCanvas=true, canvasCount=2, data-canvas-ready='true', asciiCanvasOpacity='0.3', asciiDomDisplay='block', asciiDomOpacity='0', and a mounted fallback PRE node with 32 lines / 3231 characters hidden behind the visible canvas layer.

The enhanced hero visibly shows a canvas-rendered ASCII atmosphere over the Radiant layer, while shipped code cites Hermes PAL_BOX, PAL_BLOCKS, PAL_HERMES, screen blending, FeedbackBuffer trails, and an 8fps frame interval instead of a synthetic visible DOM text grid.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output only showed the React DevTools development notice and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional network or API requests were captured during ASCII renderer validation.

assertionResults.reason: The enhanced visual stack is materially stronger than the fallback: the headed enhanced hero shows bright fluid motion plus stronger ASCII atmosphere, while the browser-side WebGL-disabled fallback becomes darker, flatter, and static with no active motion layers.

observations: Enhanced state metadata on the live hero was data-visual-state='enhanced', data-motion-layers='2', shaderCanvas=true, and asciiCanvasOpacity='0.3'.

After relaunching the same headed session with --disable-gpu,--disable-webgl before page load, fallback metadata changed to data-visual-state='fallback', data-motion-layers='0', shaderCanvas=false, and asciiCanvasOpacity='0.18'.

The visual comparison is obvious in screenshots: the enhanced hero has bright swirling Radiant depth behind the wordmark and body copy, while the fallback hero keeps the same readable composition but drops to a dimmer static grid/gradient treatment.

A direct browser screenshot diff from the fallback view against the enhanced baseline reported 16.74% pixel change (289239 / 1728000 pixels), supporting that the fallback is not a near-identical variant.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output only showed the React DevTools development notice and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional network or API requests were captured during enhanced-vs-fallback comparison.

assertionResults.reason: Headed browser evidence shows real visible background motion over time in the rendered hero itself: both the Radiant field and ASCII atmosphere shift between captured frames while the content layout stays fixed.

observations: Frame A and Frame B were captured from the same headed hero viewport 2.5 seconds apart; the BETTER wordmark and CTA block remain fixed while the blue fluid background and ASCII patterning shift around them.

A direct browser screenshot diff between the later headed hero frame and Frame A reported 10.63% pixel change (183644 / 1728000 pixels), which is consistent with visible on-page motion rather than metadata-only inference.
The headed motion recording shows the moving Radiant background and ASCII atmosphere together on the rendered page surface.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output only showed the React DevTools development notice and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional network or API requests were captured during headed motion recording.

frictions.description: To validate the degraded static fallback without changing app code, the browser had to be relaunched with --disable-gpu,--disable-webgl before page load; disabling WebGL after load would not switch the live page into fallback mode.
frictions.resolution: Closed and reopened the same required session name in headed mode with browser launch args, then recaptured the hero at scrollY=0 for the fallback comparison.

summary: Validated 4 visual-motion assertions in a headed browser session against http://127.0.0.1:3100. All 4 passed: the hero ships a traceable vendored Radiant Fluid Amber asset, the ASCII layer is a Hermes-derived real-time canvas renderer, the enhanced state is materially more dramatic than the static WebGL-disabled fallback, and headed frame/video evidence shows real visible background motion over time. No page errors were observed; console output only contained development-mode React DevTools/HMR notices.
